audit_items.proof_trace: ber_strdup(pw) at line 1476 assigns its return value directly to passwd.bv_val; line 1477 immediately calls strlen(passwd.bv_val) with no intervening NULL check. ber_strdup_x (memory.c:643) returns NULL when ber_memalloc_x fails, making this a NULL dereference if allocation fails. The if(pw) guard on line 1475 checks the input to ber_strdup, not its output.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A user runs any LDAP client tool with -W (want_bindpw), enters a password, and the process is under memory pressure causing ber_memalloc_x to return NULL; strlen(NULL) then triggers a SIGSEGV, crashing the client process.
